hero.newWay: A new way to lose weight
hero.title: Sustainable weight loss backed by medical science.
hero.description: Our program combines approved medication with personal coaching from a dedicated care team to help you achieve and sustain a healthy weight.
hero.feature1: Effective weight loss up to 25-30 kg
hero.feature2: Reliable & approved medication and top ranked health coach assistance
hero.feature3: Seamless service – no lifelong subscriptions, no hidden costs
hero.feature4: Patients report top satisfaction and lasting change
hero.feature5: Easy to follow instructions & program to save your time & money

obesity.title: Understanding obesity:
obesity.titleHighlight: It's a complex medical condition, not a choice.
obesity.description: For too long, the story around weight has been about willpower. But modern medicine shows that obesity is a complex disease, deeply influenced by your genetics, hormones, and environment. We now understand the biology behind the struggle, and with that understanding comes new, effective medical treatments that can finally help.
obesity.bodyBiologyDescription: Your body's natural "set point" is controlled by hormones. When you diet, your body often fights back by increasing hunger and slowing your metabolism, making lasting results difficult on your own.
obesity.modernMedicineDescription: Clinically-proven medications like GLP-1s work with your body's biology. Under a doctor's guidance, they help adjust your set point and reduce hunger signals, making sustainable health possible.

pricing.subtitle: An Investment In Your Long-Term Health
pricing.title: Simple,
pricing.description: We believe in being completely open about the cost of our program. There are no hidden fees or long-term commitments. Your monthly membership gives you complete access to our medical team and the support you need to succeed on your journey.
pricing.doctorFeature: Receive ongoing care from a board-certified doctor who creates your plan, manages your prescriptions, and safely guides your progress.
pricing.medicationFeature: We manage the entire prescription process, with your approved medication prescribed to you and give you a personal health care coaching.
pricing.messagingFeature: Enjoy unlimited messaging with your personal care team for continuous support, coaching, and answers to all your questions.
pricing.noCommitmentFeature: There are no binding terms for your membership.
pricing.lifestyleDescription: Your access to medical treatment and support. No obligation — cancel anytime.
pricing.medicalDescription: Your all-in-one access to our medical treatment and personal lifestyle coaching team. No obligation — cancel anytime.
pricing.promoLabel: {{discount}} off for 3 months — use code at checkout
pricing.maintenanceNote: Maintenance program pricing will be lower

features.labTestingAndMiniCheck: Lab testing and mini health check

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.helpButton: Still need help? Get in touch
faq.introduction: Our program is straightforward, transparent, and supportive. You get medical expertise, personalized coaching, and ongoing follow-up — without unnecessary costs, complicated steps, or hidden conditions. We help you save time, build knowledge, and create long-term healthy lifestyle habits that truly last.

different.question: What makes your program different from other weight loss plans?
different.answer: Our program is built on transparency, medical expertise, and personal support. There are no hidden or unnecessary costs, and no obligations around prescriptions. Every plan is individualized — combining medical treatment when needed, regular lifestyle coaching (digital, personal, or in small groups), and ongoing follow-up. We focus on making the process simple and time-saving, so you don't have to chase appointments at your local healthcare center.

dietExercise.question: Do I still need to diet and exercise?
dietExercise.answer: Yes. Medication is not a replacement for healthy habits — it's a tool that makes it easier. We'll help you find realistic food and activity strategies that fit your life, without complicated steps or restrictive programs.

meetingFrequency.question: How often will I meet with the team?
meetingFrequency.answer: We offer regular check-ins, digitally. These sessions give you feedback, allow us to adjust your treatment, and provide lifestyle coaching so you feel supported at every step.

why.question: Why do you use medication for weight loss?
why.answer: Obesity is a medical condition that involves metabolism, hormones, and the brain's hunger signals. Medication helps by reducing cravings, balancing blood sugar, and supporting fullness. This makes lifestyle changes easier and more effective.

forever.question: Will I have to take the medication forever?
forever.answer: No. Some patients benefit from long-term treatment, while others use it for a shorter time to build new habits. The average treatment time is between 6 months and 2 years. Together, we'll review your progress and adjust the plan when the time is right.

whoCanTake.question: Who can take these medications?
whoCanTake.answer: Not everyone. You need to have BMI over 30, or BMI over 27 and one risk factor for developing cardiovascular disease (such as high blood pressure, high fasting blood sugar, high blood fats, apnea disease, etc). Safety is important, so we always start with a thorough medical review to make sure the treatment is right for you.

safe.question: Are these medications safe?
safe.answer: Yes — when used under professional medical supervision. We check your health carefully before starting and follow you closely throughout treatment.

alcohol.question: Can I drink alcohol while on treatment?
alcohol.answer: In most cases, low consumption of alcohol is safe. We'll give you tailored advice based on your health profile and treatment plan.

commonSideEffects.question: What are the most common side effects of medications for weight loss?
commonSideEffects.answer:
Many people experience digestive symptoms at the start, especially when doses are increased:
• Nausea or mild vomiting
• Constipation or diarrhea
• Feeling full more quickly than usual
• Bloating or mild stomach discomfort

These usually improve as your body adjusts to the medication. We'll guide you through how to manage these effects (for example, with dietary tips and gradual dose adjustments).

lesserCommonSideEffects.question: What are the less common but important side effects?
lesserCommonSideEffects.answer:
• Gallbladder issues: Rapid weight loss can sometimes increase the risk of gallstones.
• Pancreatitis (inflammation of the pancreas): Rare, but serious. Severe abdominal pain that doesn't go away needs urgent medical attention.
• Thyroid concerns: These medications aren't suitable for people with a personal or family history of certain rare thyroid cancers — we'll review your medical history to ensure safety.
• Muscle and bone loss: Quick weight loss can sometimes affect muscle and bone. That's why we'll support you with a nutrition and exercise plan to protect them.

expectations.question: What kind of results can I expect?
expectations.answer: Results vary from person to person. Many of our patients lose 10–20% of their body weight, sometimes as much as 25–30 kg, when combining medication with lifestyle changes and regular coaching.

howSoon.question: How soon will I notice results?
howSoon.answer: Most patients feel a reduced appetite within a few weeks. Weight loss usually starts within 1–2 months and continues gradually.

regain.question: Will I gain the weight back?
regain.answer: Weight regain can happen, but our long-term coaching and step-by-step medication tapering (usually 3–6 months) help reduce that risk. The goal is to build strong, sustainable habits that keep you at your new weight.

surgery.question: Do these medications replace surgery?
surgery.answer: For many patients, medication is enough. For others, it can be an alternative to, or part of, a surgical weight management journey.

noWeightLoss.question: What if I don't lose weight on the medication?
noWeightLoss.answer: We don't stop at one option. If results are limited, we'll adjust your dose, change your plan, or explore other treatment paths together.

bmi.title: Find out your BMI

tiers.notEligible: Our medical program requires a BMI of 27 or higher. Consider consulting your regular doctor for personalized advice.
tiers.conditional: You may qualify for treatment. A consultation is needed to assess your blood pressure and run blood tests to determine eligibility.
tiers.eligible: You are a candidate for treatment! Take the next step towards a healthier you.

bmi.disclaimer: *This is a starting point for understanding your health. If you have a BMI over 27, the next step is to speak with a medical professional.

step1.title: Consultation
step1.description: Tell us about your health history in a short online form. You'll then have a private consultation with a board-certified doctor to see if you're a candidate. To evaluate your health and ensure your treatment, you will need to provide blood samples and have your blood pressure measured at a laboratory. Once the test results are ready, your doctor will get back to you with prescriptions and a treatment plan.

step2.title: Get Your Custom Plan
step2.description:
Your doctor reviews your health profile. You have two options to choose:

• Lifestyle program - Regular follow-up and training with a health coach to guide you on nutrition and workout

step3.title: Receive Treatment & Support - All the way long
step3.description:
Your dedicated care team provides ongoing support to guide you, adjust your plan, and celebrate your success.

We aim to treat you, give you the necessary support with lifestyle changes and educate you about overcoming obesity and having a healthy lifestyle. Being a part of this project - enjoy your journey losing weight and improving your health. You receive your prescription online and can start your treatment.

statistics.stat1: In Sweden, 1 in 2 adults is now classified as overweight or living with obesity.
statistics.stat2: of people who lose weight through traditional dieting alone regain it within a few years.
statistics.stat3: A 15% average weight loss with our program dramatically improves heart health and lowers diabetes risk.

partner.title: About us!
partner.subtitle: Personalized. Science-based. Designed for you.
partner.intro: Losing weight isn't just about eating less or exercising more — it's about understanding your body and giving it the right support. At Vidacure, we help you take control of your health with a proven, medical approach to weight loss.

reasons.medical: Led by medical experts: Our team of doctors and health coaches work together to give you the best care possible.
reasons.science: Science you can trust: We use evidence-based treatments, including the latest weight-loss medications, combined with practical lifestyle strategies.
reasons.personalized: Personalized care: No two journeys are the same. We listen to you and design a plan that fits your life, your goals, and your needs.
reasons.longTerm: Long-term success: We focus not just on losing weight but on helping you keep it off — with ongoing support every step of the way.

partner.promiseText:
We know that living with obesity can be overwhelming. That's why we're here — to support you, guide you, and celebrate your progress. Whether through medication, coaching, or lifestyle changes, we'll help you achieve real, lasting results.

This isn't just another weight-loss program. It's your journey to better health, backed by a team who cares and the science that works.

Welcome to join our community!
Best Regards,

selma.name: Selma Mehr
selma.title: Specialist doctor in family medicine
selma.description: As a specialist in general medicine with eight years of experience, I have broad expertise in preventive healthcare and treatment of chronic diseases. My philosophy is to focus on a holistic approach to weight loss and health, as it's more than just diet and exercise. I work with patients to improve their metabolic control, thereby preventing diseases such as type 2 diabetes, high blood pressure, high blood lipids, and cardiovascular diseases.
selma.description2: My extensive experience also includes focus on stress management, sleep, and behavioral changes to achieve lasting results. I strive to make the entire process as personal and simple as possible, also offering health coaching as a vital part of the program.
selma.programDescription:
When you begin our medical treatment program, your first step is a digital consultation with a physician. During this meeting, we review your medical history, your individual circumstances, and your personal goals. If needed, you will be referred for blood tests to ensure that all treatment decisions are based on safe and accurate medical information.

Based on your results and needs, you receive a personalized treatment plan with clear instructions to support a safe and structured dose escalation. Throughout the entire process, we remain available for questions, guidance, and ongoing support.

Our goal is to guide you step by step until you reach a healthy and sustainable weight. Once your goal is achieved, we help you taper off the treatment gradually and safely.

Lifestyle changes are essential for long-term success. If you would like additional support, we recommend our lifestyle program, where you receive professional guidance from an experienced health coach.

marcus.name: Marcus Flink
marcus.title: Health Coach
marcus.description: Marcus Flink is a trained physiotherapist with a strong interest in longevity (living well, for a long time). As a physiotherapist, Marcus has worked with many different patient groups as well as with the Swedish national surf team. As a researcher, Marcus co-authored a nationwide study comparing digital group training with traditional group training for heart attack patients. The results show that digital groups are fully feasible, safe, and provide similar experiences and effects as traditional group training.
marcus.description2: The problem is that these digital group training sessions should have been available in heart patients' lives many years earlier — then perhaps the heart attack could have been avoided. Combined with knowledge of digital groups and Marcus's experience in health coaching, health-promoting interventions can now be implemented as soon as risk factors for future heart attacks are present. This means that people with high blood pressure, obesity, and physical inactivity should, and now can, receive the help that health coaching through digital groups offers.
marcus.coachingTitle: When you choose health coaching with Marcus
marcus.coachingDescription: When you choose to participate in health coaching with Marcus, you will be part of digital group sessions every two weeks for 6 months. Each session is structured to cover bio-, psycho-, and social factors that affect your weight and health. Individual goals will be designed and followed up on. To facilitate goal achievement, a community will be created regardless of where you are geographically. You will be given opportunities to share your journey and you will notice that you are not alone. Each group session ends with some form of movement and believe it or not, it will be fun! No requirement to be visible on camera, but it can help promote group dynamics.
marcus.bctDescription: Our treatment approach is based on Behaviour Change Techniques (BCT), a structured and research-based model for behaviour change. Through goal-focused work, continuous follow-up, and group support, we create the conditions for lasting lifestyle changes and sustainable weight loss.

giedre.name: Giedre Gaizauskaite-Poe
giedre.title: Rapid Transformational Therapy® Practitioner and Coach
giedre.description: My work focuses on serving people in finding the cause of their current limitations and together with my clients transforming them into a desired lifestyle and outcome. I have 14 years of experience working as a coach, I am trained in this incredible method called Rapid Transformational Therapy® and I am a hypnotherapist. I have selected this method to create change within people because it is quite simply the best method I have come across in all my practice.
giedre.description2: Lasting weight loss starts when we address the root causes, not just the symptoms. If you're here, it's because you're ready for real change. Not a quick fix, not another short-term plan, but a lifestyle you can truly sustain — one that supports your health, your energy, and your future.
giedre.description3: Perhaps you've even struggled with your weight for years, doing "everything right" yet still feeling stuck. And deep down, you may sense that it's not only about food or exercise. Our habits are often shaped by emotions, experiences, and patterns we don't even realize we're carrying. Does that resonate with you?
giedre.description4: This is why I invite you to look beyond the surface and explore the root causes behind your current behaviors — so you can gently release what no longer serves you and create new habits that last. With the right support, you can build the conditions for lasting change and enjoy better health, confidence, and well-being.
giedre.programSubtitle: Rewire your mind for sustainable weight loss
giedre.programDescription: I have designed a Mind-Body Recode Program to help you create permanent changes so that you can get the best results on your weight loss journey. It consists of a few elements and depending on how we choose to use the time it could extend to 6–8 weeks approximately.
programIncludes: 1 tailor-made 100% individualized audio recording to reflect our session — a recording you will listen to daily
2 × 1-hour coaching sessions towards your bright future
1-hour coaching and 30-min call at the end of the cycle

partner.ctaText: Ready to start your health journey?

testimonials.testimonial1: If there is anywhere you should turn for help, it is here!! I have now lost 28 kilos and feel fitter, stronger and the self-confidence that was at rock bottom has completely turned around. Doctor Selma is knowledgeable, empathetic and a person who cares incredibly much during your health journey. You will get an incredible amount of tips, advice and a doctor who cheers you on throughout your journey.
testimonials.testimonial2: I really appreciate the clear and easy-to-understand instructions regarding the medication, which have made it simple to follow the treatment. In addition, the valuable advice on how to change my habits around diet and exercise has given me motivation and tools to improve my health in the long term. I can highly recommend Vidacure for their great commitment and support.
testimonials.testimonial3: I truly want to recommend Vidacure and my doctor, Selma. I thought surgery was my last resort for weight loss — until she encouraged me to try the medication. I needed to lose 30 kg, and within 10 months I reached my goal. I could never have done it without this support. I always get quick responses to my questions and concerns. Eternally grateful that I found my way here!
testimonials.testimonial4: I want to express my deepest gratitude for the support I have received from Dr. Selma — an exceptional and deeply insightful physician who has truly changed my life. When I first began my medical treatment, I was uncertain and had many questions, but from the very beginning, Dr. Selma was clear about what I could expect. She carefully explained that the medication is not a final solution, but rather a tool — a starting point for making sustainable changes in my lifestyle. Throughout the entire process, she has been a constant source of support and inspiration, and with her guidance, I have managed to lose 20 kg. But it's not just the weight loss that has made a difference. Dr. Selma has given me a true understanding of what it takes to make lasting changes that will carry me forward. Now, 20 kg lighter and with a completely new outlook on my health, I no longer see the treatment as the end of my journey. Thank you for everything, Dr. Selma. Thank you for believing in me — and for helping me believe in myself.

article1.title: What is Obesity?
article1.content:
Obesity is a long-term (chronic) medical condition where the body has too much fat stored. It is not just about appearance — obesity can affect your health in many different ways. Doctors today recognize obesity as a complex disease, influenced by many factors such as genetics, diet, physical activity, stress, sleep, and even certain medications.

## How is Obesity Measured?

The most common tool is the Body Mass Index (BMI).

• BMI is calculated by dividing your weight (in kilograms) by your height (in meters squared).
• For adults:
  • A BMI of 25 or higher is considered overweight.
  • A BMI of 30 or higher is considered obesity.

Other measures, such as waist circumference, can also give important information about health risks, since fat stored around the waist can increase the risk of certain diseases.

## Why Does Obesity Matter?

Carrying extra weight, especially over a long time, increases the risk of:

• Type 2 diabetes
• High blood pressure and heart disease
• Stroke
• Certain types of cancer
• Liver and kidney problems
• Joint and mobility issues

## The Good News

Even small amounts of weight loss — as little as 5–10% of your body weight — can improve blood sugar, blood pressure, and cholesterol levels, lowering the risk of serious disease.

## Treatment and Support

Obesity is not simply about willpower. It is a complex condition that often requires professional help. Safe and effective treatment options include:

• Guidance on nutrition and physical activity
• Support for sleep and stress management
• Medical treatments (including medications, if needed)
• Long-term follow-up and care

**Key Message:** Obesity is a treatable medical condition. With the right support, many people succeed in reaching healthier weight levels and improving their quality of life. You are not alone — help is available.

article2.title: Treating Obesity: What You Need to Know
article2.content:
Obesity affects the body's natural regulation of hunger and energy use. Hormones and metabolism adapt in ways that make it hard to lose weight and keep it off with diet and exercise alone. That's why medications (like GLP-1 agonists) or surgery are sometimes needed for long-term weight management.

Obesity treatment has improved a lot in recent years. New medications can help people lose much more weight than before — results that previously were only possible with surgery. The best approach usually combines several things: a healthy lifestyle, medications, and for some people, surgery.

Obesity is not just about weight. It's a long-term health condition that can affect your quality of life and increase the risk of other health problems. The good news is that new treatments, like semaglutide and tirzepatide, are helping many people reach weight loss goals that once felt out of reach.

Maintaining weight can still be challenging. That's why ongoing support, regular check-ins, and a plan tailored to you are key for long-term success.

## What Happens in the Body with Obesity

Obesity is more than "extra fat." It causes changes in hormones, the brain, and metabolism that make weight regulation harder:

### Fat Cells as Hormone Producers
• Fat cells send signals (hormones like leptin) to the brain to indicate when you are full.
• In obesity, these signals often don't work properly, so the brain may still feel hungry.

### Brain and Appetite
• Normally, the brain balances hunger and fullness. In obesity, hunger signals are stronger and fullness signals are weaker.
• The brain's reward system can make high-calorie foods even more tempting.

### The Body's "New Normal" Weight
• The body defends a higher weight. When you lose weight, hunger increases and the body conserves energy.

### Metabolic Changes
• You burn fewer calories at rest after losing weight.
• The body becomes more efficient at storing fat.

### Low-Grade Inflammation
• Fat around organs can cause chronic inflammation, affecting the heart, liver, insulin, and blood vessels.

## How Medications Help

### Stabilizing Blood Sugar
• Medications help the body use energy more efficiently and reduce fat storage.
• This leads to fewer cravings and more stable energy levels.

### Reducing Hunger
• Medications act on the brain so you feel full faster and eat less.
• They also reduce the brain's reward response to high-calorie foods.

### Slower Stomach Emptying
• Food stays in the stomach longer, keeping you full longer and helping naturally reduce portion sizes.

## Why Diet and Exercise Alone Often Aren't Enough

When you try to lose weight with just diet and exercise:

• Hunger hormones increase → you feel hungrier.
• Fullness hormones decrease → the body thinks you're still hungry.
• Metabolism slows → you burn fewer calories.

This combination of increased hunger and reduced energy use is why weight regain is common after initial lifestyle-based weight loss.

**Key Takeaway:** Obesity is a disease, not a matter of willpower. With the right support, medications, and lifestyle changes, it's now possible to lose weight and keep it off more effectively than ever before.

article3.title: How Overweight and Obesity Affect Women's Health
article3.content:
Being overweight or living with obesity can have a unique impact on women's health. It doesn't only affect body weight — it influences hormones, fertility, pregnancy, long-term health risks, and even emotional well-being. Understanding these effects can help women take steps toward prevention, treatment, and support.

## Fertility & Hormones

Carrying extra weight can interfere with the delicate balance of reproductive hormones. This often leads to:

• **Irregular periods:** Hormonal changes can disrupt the menstrual cycle, making it less predictable.
• **Reduced ovulation:** Some women may ovulate less often or not at all, which can make it harder to conceive.
• **Hormonal imbalance:** High levels of insulin and androgens (male hormones) are more common in women with obesity. These changes further disrupt reproductive health.
• **Lower IVF success rates:** Research shows that women with obesity may respond less effectively to fertility treatments such as in vitro fertilization (IVF).

## Pregnancy Risks

Obesity during pregnancy is linked with a higher risk of complications for both the mother and the baby. These include:

• **Gestational diabetes:** High blood sugar during pregnancy, which can affect both mother and child.
• **High blood pressure and preeclampsia:** Serious conditions that can threaten both maternal and fetal health.
• **Preterm birth:** Babies may be born too early, leading to potential health challenges.
• **High birth weight babies:** Larger babies can make delivery more difficult and may increase the risk of cesarean section.
• **Postpartum recovery:** Women with obesity may face longer or more complicated recovery after childbirth and a higher risk of long-term health problems.

## Other Health Risks

The effects of obesity go beyond reproductive health. Over time, the risks for chronic diseases increase:

• **Type 2 diabetes:** Obesity is a major risk factor for insulin resistance and diabetes.
• **Cardiovascular disease:** Higher blood pressure, cholesterol imbalances, and increased strain on the heart are common.
• **Certain cancers:** The risk of some cancers, such as breast cancer after menopause and endometrial (uterine) cancer, is higher.
• **Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS):** This common hormonal disorder is more frequent among women with obesity. PCOS can cause irregular periods, excess hair growth, acne, and fertility problems.

## Mental & Emotional Well-Being

The psychological impact of obesity should not be underestimated. Many women experience:

• **Lower self-esteem:** Feeling less confident in social or personal situations.
• **Higher risk of depression and anxiety:** Emotional health challenges are more common in women with obesity.
• **Body image concerns:** Social norms and pressures around appearance can intensify stress and feelings of inadequacy.

These challenges highlight the importance of not only addressing physical health but also providing emotional and psychological support.

## What You Can Do / What Helps

Managing weight and reducing health risks is not about willpower alone. It requires a combination of support, treatment, and lifestyle strategies:

• **Before pregnancy:** Working on achieving a healthier weight, eating a balanced diet, and staying active can improve fertility and reduce pregnancy complications.
• **Medical guidance:** Doctors and nutritionists can provide tailored advice, and in some cases, medications may be prescribed to help with weight management.
• **During pregnancy:** Regular check-ups to monitor blood sugar, blood pressure, and fetal development are essential.
• **Mental health support:** Counseling, group therapy, or support programs can provide encouragement and help manage stress and body image concerns.
• **Holistic care:** A long-term, personalized approach combining lifestyle changes, medical treatment, and emotional support offers the best chance for success.

## Key Takeaway

For women, being overweight or obese can affect much more than appearance — it can influence fertility, pregnancy, long-term health, and mental well-being. The good news is that with the right support and treatment, many of these risks can be reduced. Whether through lifestyle adjustments, medical help, or emotional support, women can take steps to improve both their health and their quality of life.

seo.defaultTitle: Vidacure - Sustainable Weight Loss Backed by Medical Science
seo.defaultDescription: Vidacure combines approved medication with personal coaching to help you achieve sustainable weight loss. Effective, science-backed treatment with medical support.
seo.defaultKeywords: weight loss, medical weight loss, GLP-1, obesity treatment, sustainable weight loss, healthcare Sweden, vidacure

home.title: Sustainable Weight Loss Program - Medical Support & Coaching
home.description: Achieve lasting weight loss with Vidacure's medical program. FDA-approved medication, personal coaching, and dedicated care team. Start your transformation today.

aboutUs.title: About Vidacure - Your Medical Weight Loss Partner
aboutUs.description: Meet the Vidacure team dedicated to helping you achieve sustainable weight loss through medical science, personalized coaching, and continuous support.

whatIsObesity.title: What is Obesity? Understanding the Medical Condition
whatIsObesity.description: Learn about obesity as a complex medical condition, its biological factors, and how modern medical treatments can help achieve sustainable weight management.

treatingObesity.title: Treating Obesity - Modern Medical Approaches
treatingObesity.description: Discover evidence-based obesity treatments including GLP-1 medications, lifestyle coaching, and comprehensive medical support for lasting results.

womenHealth.title: Women's Health and Obesity - What You Need to Know
womenHealth.description: Explore the unique relationship between women's health and weight management, including hormonal factors and personalized treatment approaches.

banner.title: We use cookies
banner.description: We use cookies to enhance your experience and enable appointment booking features.
banner.descriptionShort: to enhance your experience and enable appointment booking.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.description: Required for the website to function properly. Cannot be disabled.

imageAlt.heroGallery1: Patient transformation - before and after weight loss journey
imageAlt.heroGallery2: Successful weight loss transformation with Vidacure program
imageAlt.heroGallery3: Medical weight loss success story - healthy lifestyle achievement

imageAlt.journeyStep3: Step 3: Ongoing support from dedicated care team throughout weight loss journey

imageAlt.medicalInjections: GLP-1 medication for evidence-based weight management

imageAlt.doctorSelma: Dr. Selma Mehr - Specialist doctor in family medicine at Vidacure
imageAlt.coachMarcus: Marcus Flink - Health Coach at Vidacure

privacyPolicy.seoDescription: Read about how Albafides Care AB (Vidacure) processes your personal data in accordance with GDPR and the Swedish Patient Data Act.
privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.intro: Albafides Care AB, reg. no. 551929-4888, with address Stora Varvsgatan 15E, 211 77 Malmö, is responsible for the processing of personal data within the scope of our operations and digital services under the brand Vidacure. We comply with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Swedish Patient Data Act, and other relevant legislation.
privacyPolicy.dpoText: Our Data Protection Officer helps ensure that we handle personal data correctly and securely. You can contact the DPO via email: dpo@vidacure.se or by mail to Albafides Care AB, attn: Data Protection Officer, Stora Varvsgatan 15E, 211 77 Malmö.

section1.title: 1. What personal data we process
section1.intro: Personal data is information that can be directly or indirectly linked to you as an individual. This may include:
examples: Name, personal identity number, and contact details
Information about health, lifestyle, and medical history
Technical data from our digital services
section1.processingDef: Processing of personal data includes all actions relating to the data, such as collection, storage, structuring, analysis, transfer, and deletion.
section1.legalBasisIntro: To process personal data, we must have a legal basis under GDPR. For our operations, the following are primarily used:
legalBases: Contract – when you use our services
Legal obligation – e.g., record-keeping under the Patient Data Act
Legitimate interest – e.g., to improve our services
Consent – when required for specific purposes
section1.sensitiveNote: When processing sensitive personal data, such as health data, we rely on GDPR Article 9, specifically 9.2(h) (healthcare) and 9.2(a) (explicit consent).

section2.title: 2. How we process personal data in different situations

subsections.title: 2.1 When you create an account and we assess if the service is suitable for you
subsections.examples: Name, personal identity number, age, contact details, health information, lifestyle data, photos, technical data, user data.
processing: Collection of data via forms, calls, and digital services
Assessment of whether Vidacure is suitable for you
subsections.purpose: To offer an account, carry out medical assessments, and provide our digital services.

subsections.title: 2.2 When you subscribe and pay for a membership
subsections.examples: Personal identity number, name, contact details, payment information, credit assessment.
subsections.recipients: Payment providers, IT providers, BankID, credit reference agencies.

subsections.title: 2.3 When we provide care and treatment
subsections.examples: Health data, test results, medical records, lifestyle data, communication with healthcare staff, photos, user data.
processing: Communication between you, healthcare staff, and other patients (e.g., community features)
subsections.legalBasis: Contract and legal obligation under the Patient Data Act.
subsections.recipients: Laboratories, pharmacies, other healthcare providers, IT providers, prescription services, authorities when required.

subsections.title: 2.4 When we work on patient safety and quality improvement
subsections.examples: Health data, user data, incident management, injury investigations.
processing: Handling of IVO (Health and Social Care Inspectorate) cases
subsections.legalBasis: Legitimate interest and legal obligations in healthcare.

subsections.title: 2.5 When you contact customer service
subsections.examples: Contact details, personal identity number, technical data, information you provide in the matter.

subsections.title: 2.6 When we market our services
subsections.examples: Contact details, user behavior, reviews, information you provide in recommendations or "refer a friend".
subsections.legalBasis: Legitimate interest and consent where required.

subsections.title: 2.7 When we develop and improve our services
subsections.examples: Technical data, user behavior, comments, health data (which is anonymized).

items.title: 3.1 Employees and consultants
items.text: Only individuals who need the data for their work are given access.

items.title: 3.2 Data processors
items.text: We engage providers for IT operations, customer service, marketing, laboratory services, etc. With all of these, we have agreements on data protection, confidentiality, and security, ensuring they only process data according to our instructions and with a high level of protection.

items.title: 3.3 Independent data controllers
items.text: In some cases, data is shared with entities that are independently responsible for their own processing, such as authorities (Swedish Tax Agency, IVO, Swedish Social Insurance Agency, Police), pharmacies, laboratories, payment providers, and other healthcare providers. When data is shared with these parties, their own privacy policies apply.

section4.title: 4. How long we retain data
items: Medical records: 10 years under the Patient Data Act.
Contract and subscription data: during the contract period + 6 months.
Marketing data: up to 6 months after the activity ends.
Data based on consent: deleted upon withdrawal or after 2 years at the latest.
Data for development and research: anonymized.

section5.title: 5. Where data is processed
section5.text: We strive to process all personal data within the EU/EEA. If data is transferred to countries outside the EU/EEA, we ensure the level of protection through EU Commission adequacy decisions, or standard contractual clauses and other appropriate safeguards.

section6.title: 6. Your rights
rights: Receive information about how we process your data
Request access to your data (subject access request)
Object to processing based on legitimate interest
Receive your data for data portability
section6.contact: You can contact us or our Data Protection Officer to exercise your rights.
section6.complaint: You may also file a complaint with the Swedish Authority for Privacy Protection (IMY).
